assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md explicitly shows a 'Configuration Used' table with 'Project key: TC' and 'Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024'. All three JQL queries use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024', confirming these values are sourced from configuration rather than hardcoded.

assertion_results.text: Three separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged), one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New), and one for Ready for QA candidates (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status NOT IN Closed, Verified, ON_QA) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md shows three distinct JQL queries: Query 1 (Untriaged) uses 'labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged)'; Query 2 (Triaged but Still New) uses 'labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New'; Query 3 (Ready for QA) uses 'labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status NOT IN (Closed, Verified, ON_QA)'. All three match the required patterns exactly.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into three clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues, Triaged but still New issues, and Ready for QA issues
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md organizes results under three clearly separated sections with H2 headings and horizontal rule dividers: 'Query 1: Untriaged Issues' (4 issues), 'Query 2: Triaged but Still New' (1 issue), and 'Query 3: Ready for QA' (3 candidates, further split into Ready and Excluded).

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: Most issues show all five fields (e.g., 'TC-9001 | New | CVE-2026-40112 | h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2] | Created: 2026-06-08'). However, the two excluded issues in the Ready for QA section (TC-9023 and TC-9026) are listed in a table that shows Issue, Status, CVE, Summary, and Reason — but omits the Created date field. For example, TC-9023 row shows 'TC-9023 | In Progress | CVE-2026-39102 | rustls - Certificate validation bypass [rhtpa-2.1] | Remediation in progress: TC-9025 is still In Progress' with no created date.

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: status-handling.md explicitly states for TC-9001, TC-9002, and TC-9004: 'Current status: New' and 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.' For TC-9003: 'Current status: In Progress' and 'Handling: Warn the user: "This issue is already in In Progress. It may be actively worked on."' with options to proceed or skip.

assertion_results.text: Ready for QA filtering: TC-9020 appears in Ready for QA (all linked Tasks Done/Closed), TC-9023 is excluded (TC-9025 still In Progress), TC-9026 is excluded (no Depend links)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md shows TC-9020 under 'Ready for QA (1 issue)' with 'Remediation Tasks: TC-9021 (Done), TC-9022 (Closed)'. TC-9023 is listed under 'Excluded (2 issues)' with reason 'Remediation in progress: TC-9025 is still In Progress'. TC-9026 is listed under 'Excluded (2 issues)' with reason 'No linked remediation Tasks (no "Depend" links found)'. All three match the expected filtering behavior exactly.
